perturbation.original_text: In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 708,050 Shares within the period herein provided, this Agreement shall terminate and the Company shall refund to any persons who have subscribed for any of the Shares, the full amount which it may have received from them plus accrued interest as set forth in the Prospectus; and none of the parties to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other parties hereunder, except as set forth in this Section 2 and in Sections 6, 8 and 9 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 708,050 Shares within the period herein provided, this Agreement shall terminate; and none of the parties to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other parties hereunder, except as set forth in this Section 2 and in Sections 6, 8 and 9 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'the Company shall refund to any persons who have subscribed for any of the Shares, the full amount which it may have received from them plus accrued interest as set forth in the Prospectus', this creates an in-text contradiction since later on the contract speaks of refunding fees to the subscribers, specifically in Section 11 (a): 'In the event the Company fails to sell the required minimum number of the Shares by _________, 2000, and in accordance with the provisions of the Plan or as required by the MHC Regulations, and applicable law, this Agreement shall terminate upon refund by the Company to each person who has subscribed for or ordered any of the Shares the full amount which it may have received from such person, together with interest as provided in the Prospectus, and no party to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other hereunder, except for payment by the Company, the MHC and/or the Bank as set forth in Sections 2(a), 6, 8 and 9 hereof.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Agent may terminate this Agreement by giving the notice indicated below in this Section 11 at any time after this Agreement becomes effective as follows: (a) In the event the Company fails to sell the required minimum number of the Shares by _________, 2000, and in accordance with the provisions of the Plan or as required by the MHC Regulations, and applicable law, this Agreement shall terminate upon refund by the Company to each person who has subscribed for or ordered any of the Shares the full amount which it may have received from such person, together with interest as provided in the Prospectus, and no party to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other hereunder, except for payment by the Company, the MHC and/or the Bank as set forth in Sections 2(a), 6, 8 and 9 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the clause requiring the Company to refund subscribers if the minimum share sale is not met. Section 11(a), the contradicted text, explicitly states that the agreement terminates 'upon refund by the Company to each person who has subscribed for or ordered any of the Shares the full amount which it may have received from such person, together with interest'. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text removes the obligation to refund, whereas the contradicted text requires a refund for termination under the same condition, meaning it is unclear whether subscribers should be refunded if the minimum sales target is not met.

perturbation.original_text: The Agent shall receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: 3 (a) A management fee of $25,000, payable in four installments of $6,250 on November 29 and December 29, 1999 and January 29 and February 29, 2000. Should the Offering be terminated for any reason not attributable to the action or inaction of the Agent, the Agent shall have earned and be entitled to be paid fees accruing through the stage at which the termination occurred. (b) A Success Fee of $75,000.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agent shall receive the following compensation for its services hereunder: 3 (a) A management fee of $25,000, payable in four installments of $6,250 on November 29 and December 29, 1999 and January 29 and February 29, 2000. Should the Offering be terminated for any reason not attributable to the action or inaction of the Agent, the Agent shall have earned and be entitled to be paid fees accruing through the stage at which the termination occurred.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause (b) A Success Fee of $75,000, there's now an inconsistency between Section 2(a) and 6, since Section 6 states 'and the fees set forth under Section 2'. The omission of the success fee in Section 2 directly contradicts the reference to 'the fees' including it in Section 6, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Whether or not the Offering is completed or the sale of the Shares by the Company is consummated, the Company, the MHC and the Bank jointly and severally agree to pay or reimburse the Agent for the Company, the MHC and the Bank have agreed to reimburse the Agent for its out-of-pocket expenses, and its legal fees (as specified in Section 2) and to indemnify the Agent against certain claims or liabilities, including certain liabilities under the Securities Act, and will contribute to payments the Agent may be required to make in connection with any such claims or liabilities; and the fees set forth under Section 2. In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 708,050 Shares, the Company, the MHC and the Bank shall promptly reimburse the Agent in accordance with Section 2 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3(b) explicitly stated a Success Fee of $75,000. The changed text omits this clause entirely. The contradicted text in Section 6 refers to 'the fees set forth under Section 2', which originally included the Success Fee. By removing the Success Fee in Section 3, but still referencing 'the fees' including it in Section 6, there's a direct contradiction because Section 6 implies the existence of a Success Fee that is no longer present in Section 3 where the fees are actually listed. This omission creates an inconsistency and ambiguity regarding the agreed-upon compensation, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Company, the MHC and the Bank have good and marketable title to all real property and good title to all other assets material to the business of the Company, the MHC and the Bank, taken as a whole, and to those properties and assets described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus as owned by them, free and clear of all liens, charges, encumbrances or restrictions, except such as are described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, or are not material to the business of the Company, the MHC and the Bank, taken as a whole; and all of the leases and subleases material to the business of the Company, the MHC and the Bank, taken as a whole, under which the Company, the MHC or the Bank hold properties, including those described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, are in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company, the MHC and the Bank have good and marketable title to all real property and good title to all other assets material to the business of the Company, the MHC and the Bank, taken as a whole; and all of the leases and subleases material to the business of the Company, the MHC and the Bank, taken as a whole, under which the Company, the MHC or the Bank hold properties are in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'and to those properties and assets described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus as owned by them, free and clear of all liens, charges, encumbrances or restrictions, except such as are described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, or are not material to the business of the Company, the MHC and the Bank, taken as a whole', this creates a contradiction with section 7(c)(2) 'The favorable opinion, dated as of the Closing Date and addressed to the Agent and for their benefit, of the Bank's local counsel, in form and substance to the effect that, to the best of such counsel's knowledge, (i) the Company, the MHC and the Bank have good and marketable title to all properties and assets which are material to the business of the Company, the MHC and the Bank and to those properties and assets described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, as owned by them, free and clear of all liens, charges, encumbrances or restrictions, except such as are described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, or are not material in relation to the business of the Company, the MHC and the Bank considered as one enterprise
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) The favorable opinion, dated as of the Closing Date and addressed to the Agent and for their benefit, of the Bank's local counsel, in form and substance to the effect that, to the best of such counsel's knowledge, (i) the Company, the MHC and the Bank have good and marketable title to all properties and assets which are material to the business of the Company, the MHC and the Bank and to those properties and assets described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, as owned by them, free and clear of all liens, charges, encumbrances or restrictions, except such as are described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, or are not material in relation to the business of the Company, the MHC and the Bank considered as one enterprise; (ii) all of the leases and subleases material to the business of the Company, the MHC and the Bank under which the Company, the MHC and the Bank hold properties, as described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, are in full force and effect; and (iii) the Bank is duly qualified as a foreign corporation to transact business and is in good standing in each jurisdiction in which its ownership of property or leasing of property or the conduct of its business requires such qualification, unless the failure to be so qualified in one or more of such jurisdictions would not have a material adverse effect on the condition, financial or otherwise, or the business, operations or income of the Bank.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Company, MHC, and Bank have good and marketable title to properties and assets described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, free and clear of liens, except as described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus. The changed text removes this specific reference to properties and assets described in the Registration Statement and Prospectus, creating a contradiction with section (2) of the 'Contradicted Text', which relies on the original statement. The omission directly conflicts with the requirement in section (2) regarding the local counsel's opinion, rendering the bank's obligation to provide the legal opinion based on Registration Statement and Prospectus unenforceable.
